title: How to linearly interpolate between two points
task: Estimate a value between two known data points — lookup tables, calibration curves, in-between dates.

excel.note: D2 = the x to estimate; A2:A3 known x values; B2:B3 known y values.

google_sheets.note: Identical; plain FORECAST also still works.

explanation: With exactly two known points, FORECAST.LINEAR draws the straight line through them and reads off the y for your x: halfway between x=2 (y=10) and x=3 (y=20) is 15. The manual form is =B2+(D2-A2)*(B3-B2)/(A3-A2) — same answer, useful when you need to see the algebra. For a bigger table, first bracket the x with the closest-match lookup pattern, then interpolate between those two rows.
